justification: Passage states masking noise helped only at monotonous tasks: "Vid rutinartade sysslor — registrering, korrekturläsning, enklare bokföring — tycktes maskeringsljudet hjälpa", while for demanding work "vid uppgifter som krävde att man höll flera tankar i luften samtidigt ... gick skattningarna åt andra hållet". This split result matches only option B; A overstates (not all tasks), C and D are not the main finding.

Text: "Effekten var alltså villkorad, inte universell" and Selander "varnar för att sälja in brus som en billig lösning" — matching C (conditional, depends on task nature). A is contradicted (traffic "blir en kuliss man glömmer bort"), B contradicted by the warning, D describes a study method not a best-matching claim.

"One national operator after another quietly ___ its overnight services" — one withdraws a service from operation; "withdrew its overnight services" is the natural transitive collocation. A dismissed=rejected as unworthy, B declined is intransitive here, D renounced=formally give up a claim/right, none fitting the sense of discontinuing a service.

"the longer routes ran at a loss that no transport ministry seemed willing to ___ indefinitely" — to underwrite a loss is to bear/guarantee it financially, which fits "indefinitely". B condone=tolerate wrongdoing, C overturn and D dispel do not collocate with sustaining a financial loss.

"its planners expected to fill perhaps half the cabins on a good night. ___, the first season sold out weeks in advance" — the outcome contradicts the modest expectation, so "Instead" marks the reversal. A Accordingly signals consequence, C Meanwhile signals simultaneity, B Otherwise is conditional; only Instead captures the contrary-to-expectation contrast.

"the revival is a good deal more ___ than the cheering headlines suggest" — the economist notes lines "survive on public subsidy" and carriages are "decades old and costly", so the revival is more precarious (financially shaky). A auspicious is the opposite, C gregarious (sociable) and D meticulous are semantically unrelated.

"Take away the grants and the nostalgia and the ledgers look far less ___" — "the ledgers look far less rosy" is the set idiom for finances appearing less favourable once subsidy is removed. A hollow, B candid, D generous do not describe accounts and break the "outrun its arithmetic" logic.

Passage: 'Vid rutinartade sysslor — registrering, korrekturläsning, enklare bokföring — tycktes maskeringsljudet hjälpa' but 'vid uppgifter som krävde att man höll flera tankar i luften samtidigt, som att skriva en längre text, gick skattningarna åt andra hållet'. B captures the two-part result exactly; A (all tasks) is contradicted, C and D are not the main result.

Passage: 'Effekten var alltså villkorad, inte universell' and Selander 'varnar för att sälja in brus som en billig lösning'. C matches: help is conditional and depends on the nature of the work. A overstates ('alltid... aldrig' vs text's 'sällan'), B is contradicted by the conditional framing, D overgeneralizes the diary method.

Passage: rutinartade sysslor '— registrering, korrekturlaesning, enklare bokfoering — tyckt maskeringsljudet hjaelpa' medan 'vid uppgifter som kraevde att man hoell flera tankar i luften samtidigt, som att skriva en laengre text, gick skattningarna aat andra haallet'. B captures the two-part conditional result; A ('alla slags arbetsuppgifter') is contradicted; C and D describe method/subgroup, not the main result.

Passage: 'Effekten var alltsaa villkorad, inte universell' and Selander 'varnar foer att saelja in brus som en billig loesning'. C matches: small measures can help but the effect is conditional and depends on the work. A is contradicted ('sallan det hoegsta ljudet... utan det urskiljbara'; samtal stoer mer aen trafiken), B is contradicted by the conditional framing, D overgeneralizes the diary method.

'One national operator after another quietly ___ its overnight services' — to withdraw a service means to discontinue it, the exact collocation and sense here. 'declined its services' and 'dismissed its services' are ungrammatical/wrong-sense; 'renounced' is over-formal and mis-collocated. C.

'a loss that no transport ministry seemed willing to ___ indefinitely' — to underwrite a loss is to agree to bear/fund it, matching the subsidy theme ('almost all of them survive on public subsidy'). 'condone' collocates with behaviour not a loss you keep funding; 'overturn'/'dispel' a loss are wrong sense. A.

'planners expected to fill perhaps half the cabins ... ___, the first season sold out weeks in advance' — the reality contradicts the expectation, so 'Instead' is required. 'Accordingly' signals result (wrong direction), 'Meanwhile'/'Otherwise' do not carry the contrary-to-expectation contrast. D.

'Yet the revival is a good deal more ___ than the cheering headlines suggest' — 'Yet' plus the following economist's subsidy/old-carriage caveats demand 'precarious'. 'auspicious' would agree with the headlines (no contrast); 'gregarious'/'meticulous' are semantically irrelevant. B.

'Take away the grants and the nostalgia ... and the ledgers look far less ___' — removing support makes the finances look less optimistic, i.e. 'rosy' (the idiomatic 'ledgers look less rosy'). 'hollow'/'candid'/'generous' do not fit ledgers-look-less framing. C.
